stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 5 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 15 observations with mean 44.4 % (within-trial site SD = 4.1). trial site B has 15 observations with mean 31.7 % (within-trial site SD = 8.3). trial site C has 5 observations with mean 45.4 % (within-trial site SD = 8.7). trial site D has 20 observations with mean 33.6 % (within-trial site SD = 7.5). trial site E has 15 observations with mean 53.3 % (within-trial site SD = 3.3). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 3.8 and 9.6 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site E, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site E: 53.1 % (shrunk from raw 53.3). Range [52.7, 53.2] as between-trial site SD varies from 3.8 to 9.6. Pooled mean: 40.6.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 40.6 % (weighted average of 5 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site E: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=6.4: B = 0.983.
Interval [52.7, 53.2] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (53.3) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (40.6) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
